channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Ontario legislature to rise for 21-week break following short session
Over $1 billion goes to private companies administering Ontario government programs: OPSEU report
item.description: A new report authored by a public sector union whose members are striking for a second week is calling attention to how much more public money is going to private companies that administer government services.

item.title: Ontario has engaged with FIFA over alleged non-compliance with its new ticket resale law
item.description: The Ontario government says FIFA may be offside of its new law banning the resale of tickets for above face value and is warning the international soccer organization it could face a fine of up to $250,000 for doing so.

item.title: Ontario eyes boost to protections in school for kids with medical conditions
item.description: Ontario is proposing to strengthen protections for students with medical conditions such as diabetes, epilepsy, severe allergies and asthma to ensure they are safe while at school — welcome news for parents like Amy McQuaid.

item.title: Ontario in secrecy-by-default mode, critics say after blue licence plate reversal
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford’s government is in secrecy-by-default mode, opposition critics say after reviewing the content of blue licence plate documents the province had been prepared to fight the release of in the courts.

item.title: Ontario to designate Toronto island airport special economic zone after land takeover
item.description: Ontario will soon designate Toronto’s island airport as a so-called special economic zone in an effort to fast track its expansion now that the province has taken over the land, Transportation Minister Prabmeet Sarkaria said Thursday.

item.title: Vaping is Not for Kids bill advances at Queen’s Park
item.description: NDP MPP France Gélinas’s bill to curb youth vaping passed its first reading. The bill would ban promotions, restrict flavours, raise the purchase age to 21 and fund public education about product risks.

item.title: Ontario Liberal caucus member Rob Cerjanec enters party leadership race
item.description: Ontario Liberal caucus member Rob Cerjanec has tossed his hat into the party’s leadership race.

item.title: Ford government unveils framework for growing Ontario defence industry over next decade
item.description: Ontario is planning to double down on its defence sector over the next 10 years as the Liberal government in Ottawa looks to spend billions of dollars more on defence.

item.title: Ontario's solicitor general defends new powers to direct police boards
item.description: Ontario’s solicitor general is defending the new powers he is giving himself to direct police boards to follow provincial priorities.

item.title: Ontario solicitor general to review policing laws after Toronto cops charged in Spain
item.description: Ontario’s solicitor general says he will review policing laws to see if changes need to be made after the Toronto police chief highlighted that he can only suspend officers charged abroad with pay.

item.title: Queen’s Park passes bill to allow councillors to be fired for violating code of conduct
item.description: An Ottawa Liberal MPP is applauding the passage of a provincial bill that would allow municipal councillors who violate the code of conduct to be fired.

item.title: Ontario civil servants will be allowed to work from home for 2 days of World Cup with manager approval
item.description: The Ford government says managers will have the power to approve temporary work from home requests during the FIFA World Cup, but only on days when there are daytime matches in Toronto.

item.title: Navdeep Bains running for Ontario Liberal Party leader
item.description: Former federal cabinet minister Navdeep Bains wants to be the next leader of the Ontario Liberal Party (OLP).

item.title: Ontario unveils spring justice bill: Here are the highlights
item.description: The Ford government has unveiled a new spring justice bill at Queen’s Park. Here’s a rundown of some of the main points in the legislation introduced by Solicitor General Michael Kerzner.
